title: A nuclear war between India and Pakistan could destroy the ozone layer
description: Climate models suggest a small nuclear war in the tropics would do even more damage to the ozone layer than a larger nuclear war in more northerly latitudes, increasing exposure to dangerous ultraviolet radiation all over the world
author: Michael Le Page

title: Fully autonomous drones have killed human soldiers for the first time
description: A senior figure in the Ukrainian defence industry told New Scientist that a test took place two years ago involving fully autonomous drones set to destroy anything in a given area, with confirmed casualties
author: Matthew Sparkes

title: A Waymo nearly hit me, but I'm still optimistic about driverless cars
description: A near miss with a Waymo while cycling through London hasn't changed my optimistic stance on driverless cars, but we can't ever let our guard down, says Matthew Sparkes
author: Matthew Sparkes

title: Robots are about to overtake armed soldiers as the deciders of war
description: Uncrewed ground vehicles have already been tested for defending the front line by the Ukrainian military. Despite their limitations, these remotely controlled robots could be the deciding factor in many conflicts
author: David Hambling

title: Iron Age Britons may have removed the brains of the dead
description: Scrape marks inside a skull and sharpened limb bones in a set of remains found in Scotland may be evidence of unusual Iron Age funerary rituals
author: Chris Simms

title: Frozen squirrel scat preserves ancient DNA from hundreds of species
description: A complex ecosystem of woolly mammoths, bison, horses and big cats has been elucidated by studying the faeces of small rodents that probably ate the bigger animals
author: Chris Simms
